name: checkpoint-session-cap-eviction-thrash
tags: session cap, checkpoint eviction, hooks__session_id, GET /session/current, SqliteSaver, trim_checkpoints, concurrent sessions, multi-window
body:
`hooks__session_id` / `GET /session/current` returning "no checkpoint written yet" for a session that is clearly active and mid-conversation is usually NOT the hook server failing to fire for that session's cwd — it's checkpoint eviction. `_trim_checkpoints()` (hooks/server.py) keeps only the top `_CHECKPOINT_SESSION_CAP` most-recently-written thread_ids in `~/.claude/langgraph_checkpoints.db`, and runs on EVERY `UserPromptSubmit` across ALL concurrent Claude Code windows/cwds sharing the one hook server — not just at server startup. So a long-running session in e.g. SeniorDevAgent's cwd can have its checkpoint wiped mid-conversation whenever sibling sessions in other cwds (claude-hooks-dev, vault, market-intel, etc.) each submit prompts and push it out of the top-N.

Raised cap from 2 → 5 on 2026-07-05 to reduce thrash with 3+ concurrent windows open. If `hooks__session_id` starts failing "most of the time" again, check how many Claude Code windows are open concurrently before assuming a code regression — it may just mean the cap needs raising further.

**Why:** Cost real confusion this session — looked like a bug/misconfiguration for a SeniorDevAgent-cwd session, was actually eviction thrash from other concurrent sessions.

**How to apply:** When `hooks__session_id` fails, check `_CHECKPOINT_SESSION_CAP` in hooks/server.py against how many Claude Code windows are typically open at once, rather than assuming the session/cwd isn't being tracked. The concept store entry `server-fastapi-hook-server` (module hooks/server.py) previously had a stale/wrong invariant claiming eviction happens at SessionEnd, not on every UserPromptSubmit — corrected 2026-07-05.
